text: Hey Shanthi! Quick question — Waffles has been pulling really hard on her morning walks. Is there anything I should be doing differently between sessions?

Hey! Great timing — we've actually been working on loose-leash walking in her sessions. The biggest thing is consistency: every single time she pulls, stop completely. Don't take another step until there's slack in the leash. It feels slow but it works fast 🐾
Ok that makes sense. She just gets SO excited when she sees other dogs on walks lol

Ha, classic Waffles! Try turning around and walking the other direction when she spots a dog. It interrupts the excitement spiral before it starts. She'll figure out that pulling makes the fun dog go away 😄
That's genius. I'll try it tonight. Also — she's coming in Friday, right?
Yes, Friday with Ashe! They work really well together ❤️
Perfect. Can't wait to see the report card 🐶
She did amazing today, you're going to love the report card 🐾
Hi Allison! Just wanted to follow up on Brodie's reactivity session last week. How has he been on walks since?
Actually really good! He barked at one bike but I used the U-turn and he refocused on me within about 5 seconds. Huge improvement from before.

That's amazing progress!! 5 seconds of recovery is genuinely impressive for a dog with his history. Keep doing exactly that — the U-turn plus treat when he refocuses. You're rewarding the recovery, not the bark.
Got it. Can we book another session Thursday? I want to work on the moving bike thing.
Sounds good, I'll have everything set up for Brodie's session Thursday.

Hey Allison — quick note from today's session. Waffles is doing great overall but she was a little slow to respond to her name when there were other dogs nearby. Worth practicing "name game" at home: say her name, when she looks at you, party! Even just 5 minutes a day makes a big difference.
Oh interesting, she's usually really good with her name at home. Is it just the distraction level?

Exactly — her name recognition is great in low-distraction settings but falls apart when she's excited. That's normal! We just need to build the value of her name in distracting environments. Also tried her on a slow feeder bowl during lunch today — she seemed to enjoy the puzzle element of it!
Ha, she would love that. She inhales her food so fast. Does the school have one I can borrow to try?
The slow feeder is definitely helping — Waffles didn't gulp her lunch today at all.
